meta.title: Update From Solana Mobile: The Seeker Economy

transcript:
Hey everyone. Uh, I'm Emmett, General Manager of Solana Mobile. Uh, and based on what I heard backstage, it sounds like most of you either already own a Seeker or at least know what Seeker is. But for those of you that don't, um, Seeker is our definitive Web3 mobile device. Um, you know, we set out to build something that for people who really care about crypto but don't have access to the security that they need or the usability that they need or the access to apps that they need, um, we wanted to solve those problems for users. And so, last time I was on stage, uh, Seeker had been announced but we hadn't yet started shipping. And so I think at that time there were understandably some open questions from the community. You know, could we deliver on this idea of a super seamless UX? Uh, could we build hardware that people actually liked, that wasn't just a novelty? And would developers show up? Would there be apps that people actually wanted to use? Um, it was a leap of faith, and 150,000 of you took that leap with us, and the response has been incredible. Uh, people love their Seekers. They love the piece of mind of having their assets protected by Seed Vault. They love the seamless, kind of magical signing experience of Seed Vault Wallet. They love having access to all of the great apps on Solana anywhere from the Solana dApp Store. And they love the hardware. They love having a device that's just a joy to hold and a joy to use. So, 2025 really has been a breakthrough year for us. Um, and that's in no small part due to the users and developers who are really making this ecosystem go. But as we look into 2026, we really want to keep that momentum going, and not just keep it going but accelerate it. We want more users, we want more developers, and of course we want more hardware partners.

With Seeker season in full swing, we've seen over 225 dApps launch in the Solana dApp Store. Uh, and amongst them we've seen more than 1.8 billion in transaction volume in just a few short months. So if you're an established Solana team who's just looking to scale your user base even further, or a team still in search of product market fit, or a brand new team shipping your first product, Seeker really is the most obvious launchpad in crypto to get off the ground. Moonwalk Fitness, for example, has seen over 27,000 downloads since launching on Seeker, uh, and has hit all-time highs in daily active usage. Perena has seen a third of its overall user base come from Seekers, and those users collectively hold over 13 million in assets on the protocol. And Crypto Fantasy League was a team that submitted to the first Solana Mobile hackathon, and in a matter of weeks went from submission to top featured app in the dApp Store, uh, and thousands and thousands of downloads. So clearly this flywheel, this idea of developers bringing on users bringing on developers, is in full swing.

But the next step for us is expanding to additional hardware. So we're excited to be working with our partner in FX Technology to bring the Solana Mobile platform to more devices. Together we're going to start with one of the most important problem spaces in tackling the hardware market, which is the chipset. So alongside FX Technology, we'll be working closely with MediaTek and its TEE partner Trustonic to package up the whole Solana Mobile stack and make it available on additional Android devices. This is a pretty big deal. You know, for those who maybe don't know as much about the Android market or the smartphone market, um, MediaTek controls or ships about 46 to 50 percent of all Android devices globally. So that's like 2 billion phones each year. Um, and for us, you know, getting the opportunity to bring the Solana Mobile stack to MediaTek's hardware environment is a pretty clear signal to the industry that mobile and crypto are really ready to take that next step together. So, you know, we're excited to be working with those partners, we're in the process of building out these prototypes, and in parallel we're having conversations and negotiations with some of the world's largest hardware manufacturers about bringing the power of Solana to their devices and their users around the world.

This next phase is really how we go from a Solana-first kind of grassroots project to something much, much, much bigger. One that competes with the existing mobile giants kind of on their ground but on crypto's terms. But as we scale, it's really important that Solana Mobile doesn't just kind of stay the central gatekeeper. You know, if we just build the same thing but it's a little crypto friendly, it's just another walled garden. And that simply doesn't work. So back at Accelerate, we announced what we called TEEPIN. To put it simply, TEEPIN is just a different network architecture that brings phones and users and hardware manufacturers all in one place. But rather than having one central authority guarantee trust and security and safety, we bring together new novel technology and a network of what we call Guardians to ensure that trust and safety. The best way to think about Guardians is kind of like validators on the Solana network, but instead of validating transactions, our Guardians are responsible for ensuring that apps aren't malicious, uh, that devices haven't been compromised, and that the overall mechanics and integrity of our ecosystem can continue.

And I'm thrilled to announce that Helius, DoubleZero, Anza, Triton, and Jito have committed to launching as our first Guardians in 2026. And not only that, we want to expand beyond this group. So if you're out in the audience or listening at home and are interested in helping us secure this growing ecosystem, please reach out to our team. We'd love to have you. So we've got an architecture coming together. Things are opening up. But we need a way to coordinate. You know, an open platform really does need open incentives. If we think about one of the things that makes crypto great, it's the idea that you can both decentralize and align incentives across multiple parties who might have totally different motivations. And that's what we're building, right? Our ecosystem is made up of a bunch of independent parties, whether it's users who want access to the latest and greatest apps, or developers who want distribution and want the opportunity to monetize their product, or hardware manufacturers who just want to build and ship hardware, they want to differentiate and they want to create network effects. Um, we have to find a way to align the incentives of three totally independent groups of people who are all sort of participating in the same ecosystem. Uh, and the launch of SKR gives us that opportunity.

So when SKR launches, it is that coordination mechanism. It's the way we keep all of these independent ecosystem participants on the same page. And not only that, but as this ecosystem continues to scale, SKR ensures that value flows back to the community. Uh, it really is the fuel that keeps that flywheel spinning. And we think our token distribution tells you everything you need to know kind of about how we're thinking about this. We've earmarked 30% for community airdrops. 10% has been reserved for future community treasury proposals. And 25% has been set aside for a growth and partnerships fund that will go directly into the hands of dApp and hardware builders who are going to help us scale.

That builder community is super important to us. We wouldn't be here without them. Um, we want to give them the support that they need, whether it's resources or tooling or access. And so we're expanding the Solana Mobile Builder Grants program. Um, whether you're building infrastructure or tooling or you're building the next great consumer dApp, we want to help, we want to provide that funding. Uh, so please head to solanamobile.com/grants and apply now. Um, but beyond grants, we've also committed to hackathons. You know, last year we launched our first hackathon, uh, and the results have been tremendous. We've seen tons of teams launch, launch their first products, find adoption. And so we're excited to announce that we'll have our second hackathon in Q1 of 2026. And the hackathons, you know, in addition to having prize winnings and having your name out on Twitter, there's a lot that comes with that. There's featured dApp Store placement, uh, there's all the co-marketing and mindshare that we're able to help these teams secure. But there's also access to our team. You know, we've got a team of experts, technical experts, marketing experts, operational experts. Uh, and we're excited to bring access to those resources and our top phone salesman, Anatoly Yakovenko, to hackathon winners.

Phones are how people connect to the world, and crypto is increasingly becoming how value moves through it. Uh, those two things, they belong together, not apart. Uh, and in 2025, we've taken a very critical step on that path. We've got 150,000 devices, we've got hundreds of dApps, we've got billions in transaction volume. There's a very obvious road ahead for global scale. Um, but that's been made real by everyone who's a part of the community. The users and developers, they've really made this possible. So a huge thank you to everybody who's been a part of this so far. Um, if you're not one of those people, if you're somebody who bought a phone that we sent to you and it's sitting in a drawer somewhere, I'm not exactly sure what you're waiting for, but please turn it on. Uh, I promise you're missing out. Activate your phone, claim your Genesis token. Um, and if you're not one of those people, if you're still waiting, head to solanamobile.com. We would love to sell you a Seeker, or swing by our booth and try and win one.

SKR is going to launch in January. Guardians will be coming online in 2026. Uh, and we'll have many new opportunities to scale through new hardware partners. So 2025 for us really has been a year where we proved that Web3 and mobile belong together. And 2026 is when Solana Mobile becomes something truly inevitable. Thank you.

enrichment.generated_query: Solana Mobile Seeker official announcement blog OR technical docs Solana Breakpoint 2025

sources.title: Solana Mobile Blog
sources.snippet:
Solana Mobile Blog
[![Solana Mobile Logo](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68fab532460012b81e435501/68fab532460012b81e435533_Solana%20Mobile.svg)](https://solanamobile.com)
[Home](https://solanamobile.com)[Seeker](https://solanamobile.com/seeker)[SKR](https://solanamobile.com/skr)[Developers](https://docs.solanamobile.com/)[Grants](https://solanamobile.com/grants)[Blog](https://www.solanalabs.com/)[Order Now](https://store.solanamobile.com/)
From Saga to Seeker: Solana's Mobile's Smartphone Revolution
After the breakout success of Solana Saga, here's how Solana Mobile's next device —Seeker —is taking web3 mobile global.
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68fab532460012b81e43552a/68fab532460012b81e43556b_cropped-Screenshot%202025-01-15%20at%2010.44.45%E2%80%AFAM.png)
*By*[*Emmett Hollyer*](https://x.com/m_it)*
‍*
When[Solana Mobile](https://solanamobile.com/)launched its first device in 2023 —Solana Saga —web3 mobile was a huge unknown. Skeptics questioned whether the world needed another smartphone manufacturer. Traditional tech reviewers dismissed it, with MKBHD famously labeling Saga the "biggest bust of 2023."
‍But then, things changed. BONK happened. Rewards happened. It was like the entire Solana community came together to get behind the mission of web3 mobile. We sold over 15,000 devices in a single day. The Solana Saga*sold out.*
‍As we prepare to launch our second device —[Solana Seeker](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MjQrbh4gaQM)— in mid-2025, it's become clear that we aren't just building another phone. We're building the infrastructure that will make cryptocurrency accessible and practical for everyday mobile users. And with over Seeker 150,000 preorders waiting to get in the hands of the Solana community already, that new paradigm is getting closer to reality every day.
‍The crypto landscape has long been defined by compromise when it comes to UI. Users toggle between multiple apps, navigate clunky interfaces, and accept subpar mobile experiences as the cost of participating in Web3.
‍Meanwhile, the existing duopoly of Apple and Google's app stores has created an environment where crypto applications struggle to reach users, hampered by restrictive policies and prohibitive revenue shares that scrape 30% off the top of all app revenues.
‍Our journey with Saga proved there was a better way. Even when initial sales were modest during the bear market of 2023, something remarkable happened: developers kept building. Teams who saw the potential of mobile-first crypto experiences created applications specifically for the Solana Mobile dApp store. When the market turned, Saga didn't just sell out, it validated our thesis that users were hungry for a mobile device that put crypto first.
‍Now, with Seeker, we're taking that vision global. Available worldwide at a more accessible $500 price point, Seeker represents the evolution of our initial concept. It's lighter, brighter, packed with new features like the Seed Vault Wallet and an updated dApp Store. But perhaps more importantly, it's a gateway to an ecosystem that's maturing at a phenomenal pace across digital assets, payments, DeFi, DePIN, AI, and gaming —and whatever the Solana ecosystem dreams up next.
‍[![Embedded YouTube video](https://img.youtube.com/vi/UHzdMjSwVvo/0.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UHzdMjSwVvo)
‍The heart of this ecosystem is our dApp store, where developers keep 100% of their revenue –a stark contrast to the 30% taken by Apple and Google. This isn't just about developer economics; it's about new business models that can reward users directly. When developers don't have to give away a third of their revenue to platform gatekeepers, they can create innovative reward systems and incentive structures that benefit their communities.
‍We're also addressing one of the most persistent challenges for web3 mobile: wallet integration. Our new Seed Vault

sources.title: Solana Mobile Blog
sources.snippet:
Solana Mobile Blog
[![Solana Mobile Logo](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68fab532460012b81e435501/68fab532460012b81e435533_Solana%20Mobile.svg)](https://solanamobile.com)
[Home](https://solanamobile.com)[Seeker](https://solanamobile.com/seeker)[SKR](https://solanamobile.com/skr)[Developers](https://docs.solanamobile.com/)[Grants](https://solanamobile.com/grants)[Blog](https://blog.solanamobile.com/)[Order Now](https://store.solanamobile.com/)
Seeker Season Starts September 8th
Featured dApps, exclusive rewards, and onchain experiences only available on Solana Mobile.
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68fab532460012b81e43552a/68fab532460012b81e435597_seeker%20season%20blog%20header.jpg)
You ordered. You waited. And now, it’s finally time.**Seeker Season officially begins September 8th.**
‍From the initial Chapter 2 announcement to watching preorder numbers climb past 150,000 devices worldwide, you've been part of the journey as early Web3 mobile adopters. Whether you've already unboxed your Seeker or you're still waiting for your device to arrive, you've helped build the foundation for what comes next. Now comes the exciting part: putting it all to work.
‍[![Embedded YouTube video](https://img.youtube.com/vi/Lo4cBs8jQFE/0.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Lo4cBs8jQFE)
‍## ‍**What Is Seeker Season?**
Seeker Season is the catalyst for the next generation of onchain mobile apps, and it's all happening in the Solana Mobile ecosystem. Starting September 8th, we're launching a program of featured dApps, rewards, and exclusive onchain mobile experiences built specifically for Seeker users every week.
### **Each week brings new opportunities like:**
**Special rewards**from ecosystem partners
**Early access**to cutting-edge applications
**Exclusive features**available only through the Solana dApp Store
**Enhanced benefits**that leverage your Genesis Token and Seeker ID
‍You can find each week's Seeker Season activations in the Solana dApp Store. Think of it as your command center during Seeker Season, showcasing the breadth of innovation happening across the Solana ecosystem.**‍**
‍‍## **Seeker Season Activations**
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68fab532460012b81e43552a/68fab532460012b81e4355be_Solana%20Mobile%20-%2062.png)
*There are already over 100 dApps on the Solana dApp Store —with many more on the way!*
‍**DeFi and Trading**: Enhanced yields, exclusive access, and boosted rewards that recognize your verified Seeker status.
**Gaming and Entertainment**: Unique in-game experiences, early access to new releases, and exclusive content across mobile games and entertainment platforms.
**DePIN and Infrastructure**: Enhanced participation rewards, priority access, and exclusive benefits on location-based applications that put your Seeker to work.
**Social and Commerce**: Verified Seeker privileges, exclusive content and features, and special benefits across social platforms, payment apps, and creator tools.
Each week during Seeker Season will feature applications from among these categories and more, providing diverse opportunities to explore the full breadth of the Solana ecosystem.
‍## **Essential Seeker Setup Steps**
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68fab532460012b81e43552a/68fab532460012b81e4355c3_Screenshot%202025-08-12%20at%2010.35.26%E2%80%AFAM.png)
*To get the most out of Seeker Season, make sure your device is set up and you're familiar with the Solana dApp Store.*
‍**Seed Vault Wallet:**Set up your native wallet and ensure your fingerprint authentication works smoothly. This becomes your gateway to every application and exclusive experience.
**Claim Your Seeker ID:**Choose your .skr domain. This human-readable address represents your device across the entire ecosystem.
**Seeker Genesis Token:**Verify that you've claimed your unique Genesis Token. It serves as your verification credential for exclusive features and rewards throughout Seeker Season.
**Fund Yo

tags.impact_reason: The update highlights the growth of the Solana dApp store and the success of the Seeker phone, but it is not ecosystem-altering and is more of a solid update on the consumer front.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a silicon wafer/chipset as the Semantic Anchor because the story's core is Solana Mobile's strategic shift from a single hardware device (Seeker) to embedding its crypto stack directly into MediaTek's silicon. This represents the move to a foundational, scalable level targeting mass adoption. I included the diegetic text '2B' because it's the most high-impact figure from the headline, representing the 2 billion Android devices they aim to reach, and visually reinforces the theme of immense scale.
visual_idea.description: A pristine, circular silicon wafer is held by a minimalist robotic arm in a sterile, dark clean room. The wafer is the central focus, its surface etched with intricate, glowing circuits in Solana's signature green and purple. These pathways of light pulse with energy. In the center of the wafer, the glowing circuits converge to form the diegetic text '2B', appearing as if it's the core processing unit. The background is softly out of focus, with volumetric beams of light cutting through the clean air, creating depth and a sense of immense, futuristic scale.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A pristine, circular silicon wafer, glowing with internal energy, is held with precision by a sleek, minimalist robotic arm crafted from dark, anodized metal, catching subtle reflections of the environment. The scene unfolds in a sterile, dark clean room, filled with dramatic volumetric beams of light that slice through the pristine air, creating immense depth and a sense of futuristic scale. The background is softly out of focus, emphasizing the wafer as the monumental central focus. Its surface shimmers with iridescent silicon, etched with intricate, hyper-detailed glowing circuits. These luminous pathways radiate in Solana's signature vibrant green and ethereal purple, pulsing with internal energy like a living seed crystal. In the precise center of this powerful wafer, the glowing circuits converge to form the diegetic text '2B', appearing as the very core processing unit, a nexus of light and information. Composed for a vertical 3:4 aspect ratio, emphasizing epic height and monumental scale, with generous negative space above the wafer, suggesting vastness and technological advancement. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, physically based rendering, cinematic lighting, ultra-photorealistic.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a silicon wafer/chipset as the Semantic Anchor because the story's core is Solana Mobile's strategic shift from a single hardware device (Seeker) to embedding its crypto stack directly into MediaTek's silicon. This represents the move to a foundational, scalable level targeting mass adoption. I included the diegetic text '2B' because it's the most high-impact figure from the headline, representing the 2 billion Android devices they aim to reach, and visually reinforces the theme of immense scale.
visual_idea.description: An extreme macro, top-down shot focusing on the intricate patterns of a silicon chipset. We see the iridescent, multi-colored surface of the silicon in sharp detail. Luminous green and purple lines of light, representing data, flow through the microscopic pathways and transistors. The depth of field is incredibly shallow, blurring the surrounding circuits into an abstract, beautiful technological tapestry. The image is purely about the texture and energy of the technology at its most fundamental level.

final_prompt.prompt_text: An extreme macro, top-down shot focusing on the intricate patterns of a silicon chipset. The composition is perfectly symmetric, with a central focus, tightly framed to fill the entire square aspect ratio (1:1) with dense, technological texture. We see the iridescent, multi-colored surface of the silicon in incredibly sharp detail, illuminated by precise volumetric clean room lighting and soft commercial product lighting with global illumination and even light distribution. Luminous green and purple lines of light, representing data streams, vividly flow through the microscopic pathways and transistors, appearing physically integrated into the silicon structure. The depth of field is incredibly shallow, blurring the surrounding circuits into an abstract, beautiful technological tapestry with subtle anodized metal textures, emphasizing the pristine and fundamental nature of the technology. The image is purely about the texture and energetic essence of the technology at its most fundamental level, rendered with ambient occlusion. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, physically based rendering (PBR), ultra-detailed, extreme ultra detail, studio quality.

editorial.headline: Solana Mobile Targets 2B Android Devices via MediaTek Chipset Integration
editorial.standfirst: The "Seeker" device was just a proof of concept. By embedding its crypto stack into MediaTek's silicon, Solana aims to unlock Web3 capabilities for 50% of the global smartphone market.
key_takeaways: Scale: A strategic partnership with FX Technology and MediaTek aims to integrate the Solana Mobile Stack into chipsets powering ~2 billion devices annually.
Infrastructure: The new TEEPIN architecture decentralizes app store security via "Guardians" like Helius, Jito, and Anza.
Tokenomics: The SKR governance token launches in January 2026, allocating 30% of supply to community airdrops and 25% to growth.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
Solana Mobile is fundamentally altering its strategy from a niche hardware manufacturer to a mass-market platform provider. While the **Seeker** device has successfully validated the market—shipping 150,000 units and processing over $1.8 billion in transaction volume—the company’s new roadmap focuses on the 2 billion Android devices shipped annually by other manufacturers. By partnering with **FX Technology**, **MediaTek**, and **Trustonic**, Solana Mobile plans to embed its proprietary crypto-native stack directly into the chipset layer, effectively allowing any OEM using MediaTek silicon to toggle on institutional-grade Web3 security without redesigning their hardware.

This move addresses the primary bottleneck in mobile crypto adoption: the hardware duopoly. Rather than competing directly with Samsung or Xiaomi, Solana is positioning its **Seed Vault** technology as a value-add integration for the 46-50% of the global Android market powered by MediaTek. The integration leverages Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) at the silicon level, ensuring that private key management is isolated from the operating system—a security standard previously limited to flagship devices with custom secure elements.

To prevent becoming a centralized gatekeeper itself, the company introduced **TEEPIN** (Trusted Execution Environment Physical Infrastructure Network). This decentralized architecture replaces the traditional app store review board with a network of "Guardians"—independent entities responsible for verifying device integrity and screening malicious applications. Launch partners for the Guardian network include high-profile infrastructure teams such as **Helius**, **DoubleZero**, **Anza**, **Triton**, and **Jito**, ensuring that trust is distributed rather than consolidated.

The ecosystem will be coordinated by the **SKR** token, set to launch in January 2026. Designed as an incentive layer for hardware manufacturers, developers, and users, the token's distribution model heavily favors ecosystem participants, with 30% reserved for community airdrops and 25% for a growth fund. This economic alignment is critical for convincing third-party OEMs to adopt the Solana Mobile Stack, creating a flywheel where hardware partners are financially rewarded for opening their devices to the Solana ecosystem.
